specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI reasoning effort mapper translates the internal thinking level enumeration to the API-specific reasoning effort string required by each OpenAI model, accounting for model-specific restrictions on available effort values.

scope.in-scope: The set of models classified as reasoning-capable, the per-model effort level string lookup table, and the Responses API model classification table.
scope.boundary: Which API endpoint (Chat Completions or Responses API) is used for each model is covered by the OpenAI API routing specification. This topic covers only the reasoning effort string lookup.
Whether a model supports thinking at all is determined by the model capability registry. This topic specifies the effort string for models that do support reasoning.

field.name: min
field.note: minimum reasoning; maps to 'none', 'minimal', or lowest available

field.name: high
field.note: maximum reasoning; may map to 'xhigh' on some models

behavior.reasoning-model-identification:
The following models are classified as reasoning-capable: - o1, o1-mini, o1-preview - o3, o3-mini, o3-pro - o4-mini - gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano, gpt-5-pro - gpt-5.1, gpt-5.1-chat-latest, gpt-5.1-codex, gpt-5.1-codex-mini - gpt-5.2, gpt-5.2-chat-latest, gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.2-pro
Models not in this list are not reasoning models and do not use effort strings.
behavior.per-model-effort-mapping:
Each model has a distinct effort string for each thinking level. The mapping reflects API constraints that vary by model:
**o1, o1-mini, o1-preview** (reasoning cannot be disabled): - min → "low" (lowest supported value; "none" rejected by API) - low → "low" - med → "medium" - high → "high"
**o3, o3-pro, o4-mini** (full range including disable): - min → "none" - low → "low" - med → "medium" - high → "high"
**o3-mini** (same as o3): - min → "none" - low → "low" - med → "medium" - high → "high"
**gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano** (minimum is "minimal", not "none"): - min → "minimal" - low → "low" - med → "medium" - high → "high"
**gpt-5-pro** (always uses maximum reasoning): - min, low, med, high → "high"
**gpt-5.1, gpt-5.1-codex, gpt-5.1-codex-mini** (supports "none"): - min → "none" - low → "low" - med → "medium" - high → "high"
**gpt-5.1-chat-latest** (adaptive reasoning; API rejects other values): - min, low, med, high → "medium"
**gpt-5.2, gpt-5.2-codex** (supports extended "xhigh"): - min → "none" - low → "low" - med → "medium" - high → "xhigh"
**gpt-5.2-chat-latest** (adaptive reasoning; API rejects other values): - min, low, med, high → "medium"
**gpt-5.2-pro** (no disable, extended top): - min → "medium" - low → "medium" - med → "high" - high → "xhigh"
Each model family imposes different constraints on which effort values the API accepts. The mapping encodes these restrictions to prevent API rejection errors. For example, older o1 models cannot disable reasoning (so "min" maps to "low"), while some gpt-5 models require "minimal" rather than "none" as the minimum value.
behavior.responses-api-classification: A separate lookup determines whether a model uses the Responses API or the Chat Completions API. All reasoning models listed above use the Responses API. Models not in the reasoning model table use the Chat Completions API by default.
